estimate.narrative_context: Marcus only — current-year federal tax savings on $32,500 deferral at assumed 37% marginal rate. Catherine not on payroll ($0 W-2), so household combined deferral not available until REC-TAX-003 spousal payroll executes.

scenario_range.computation_method: Low = Marcus only ($32,500 × 37%). Midpoint = Marcus + Catherine partial payroll (e.g., $50K W-2 supports ~$25K deferral). High = both spouses fully maxed at $32,500 each ($65,000 × 37% = $24,050; rounded).

estimate.narrative_context: Net annual economic benefit (owner tax savings minus non-owner employee allocation cost) for cash-balance plan layered on existing 401(k). Per-rec-file Worked Example for owner age 52.

computation_inputs.rationale_employee_cost_caveat: Rec file worked example assumes $500K non-owner payroll → $30K-$40K. HIS has 87 FTE; non-owner payroll is materially larger, which could push employee carry-along cost well above the rec-file's worked-example range and compress net benefit. Final design requires actuarial workforce analysis.
computation_inputs.transaction_caution: Rec file specifies plan must run 3-5 years; HIS transaction window is 3-5 years — borderline. Flagged Active-Cautioned.

scenario_range.computation_method: Low = workforce carry-along makes net benefit zero or negative (87 FTE risk). Midpoint = rec-file worked example ($60K). High = optimal cross-testing design with owner-favorable allocation and minimal employee cost ($110K gross tax savings).
